meaning_english: Meaning: Brisk and cheerful readiness. Opposite: Apathy, Reluctance, Indifference, Hesitation
Meaning: Deviating from what is standard or normal. Opposite: Regular, Normal, Typical, Conventional
Meaning: Characterized by severe self-discipline and abstention. Opposite: Hedonistic, Sybaritic, Indulgent, Luxurious
Meaning: Showing great care and perseverance. Opposite: Lazy, Negligent, Idle, Indolent
Meaning: To give a false impression of; to contradict. Opposite: Reveal, Betray, Justify, Prove
Meaning: Noisy, energetic, and cheerful. Opposite: Quiet, Restrained, Calm, Serene
Meaning: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds. Opposite: Euphony, Harmony, Silence, Melody
Meaning: To reprimand someone severely. Opposite: Praise, Extol, Commend, Laud
Meaning: Cautiously or suspiciously reluctant to do something. Opposite: Rash, Careless, Bold, Confident
Meaning: Clear, logical, and convincing. Opposite: Weak, Unconvincing, Incoherent, Irrelevant
Meaning: Wicked and cruel; cowardly. Opposite: Heroic, Noble, Brave, Gallant
Meaning: Polite submission and respect. Opposite: Contempt, Disrespect, Defiance, Scorn
Meaning: Lacking a plan, purpose, or enthusiasm. Opposite: Systematic, Methodical, Purposeful, Focused
Meaning: Light, delicate, and translucent. Opposite: Opaque, Thick, Heavy, Cloudy
Meaning: Shy because of a lack of self-confidence. Opposite: Confident, Bold, Assertive, Arrogant
Meaning: To cause someone to feel drained of energy. Opposite: Energize, Invigorate, Strengthen, Vitalize
Meaning: Lasting for a very short time. Opposite: Eternal, Perpetual, Lasting, Enduring
Meaning: Mental calmness and composure, especially in a difficult situation. Opposite: Anxiety, Agitation, Excitement, Turmoil
Meaning: Having or showing great knowledge or learning. Opposite: Ignorant, Illiterate, Uneducated, Unlearned
Meaning: Intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people. Opposite: Common, Public, Obvious, Familiar
Meaning: To show or declare that someone is not guilty of wrongdoing. Opposite: Incriminate, Accuse, Convict, Blame
Meaning: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail. Opposite: Careless, Lax, Indifferent, Easygoing
Meaning: Silly and pointless. Opposite: Sensible, Wise, Intelligent, Serious
Meaning: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. Opposite: Taciturn, Reticent, Silent, Quiet
Meaning: A lengthy and aggressive speech. Opposite: Panegyric, Praise, Whisper, Tribute
Meaning: A person who attacks cherished beliefs or institutions. Opposite: Conformist, Believer, Traditionalist, Follower
Meaning: Having little or no money. Opposite: Wealthy, Affluent, Rich, Prosperous
Meaning: In an initial stage; beginning to happen or develop. Opposite: Final, Developed, Mature, Concluding
Meaning: Not able to be corrected, improved, or reformed. Opposite: Repentant, Curable, Reformable, Obedient
Meaning: Lacking flavor or interest; boring. Opposite: Tasty, Piquant, Exciting, Flavorful
Meaning: Tending to talk a great deal; talkative. Opposite: Taciturn, Reticent, Brief, Laconic
Meaning: Very generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival. Opposite: Mean, Petty, Stingy, Vindictive
Meaning: Not telling the truth; lying. Opposite: Truthful, Honest, Veracious, Sincere
Meaning: Showing great attention to detail; very careful. Opposite: Careless, Sloppy, Negligent, Hasty
Meaning: The lowest point in the fortunes of a person or organization. Opposite: Zenith, Peak, Apex, Pinnacle
Meaning: Obedient or attentive to an excessive degree. Opposite: Domineering, Arrogant, Assertive, Rebellious
Meaning: Involving an amount of effort that is oppressively burdensome. Opposite: Easy, Effortless, Light, Simple
Meaning: The presence of something only in small or insufficient quantities. Opposite: Abundance, Plenty, Surfeit, Excess
Meaning: Deceitful and untrustworthy. Opposite: Loyal, Faithful, Trustworthy, Devoted
Meaning: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically. Opposite: Idealistic, Impractical, Visionary, Romantic
Meaning: Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical. Opposite: Practical, Pragmatic, Realistic, Sensible
Meaning: Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily. Opposite: Outgoing, Communicative, Loquacious, Talkative
Meaning: Health-giving; healthy. Opposite: Unhealthy, Insalubrious, Noxious, Harmful
Meaning: Tending to induce drowsiness or sleep. Opposite: Stimulating, Invigorating, Exciting, Alert
Meaning: Behaving as though one is superior to others. Opposite: Humble, Modest, Respectful, Meek
Meaning: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little. Opposite: Garrulous, Loquacious, Talkative, Outgoing
Meaning: Always right or accurate. Opposite: Fallible, Erring, Faulty, Mistaken
Meaning: Bitter and abusive language. Opposite: Praise, Adulation, Commendation, Eulogy
Meaning: Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers. Opposite: Reckless, Trusting, Careless, Rash
Meaning: The time at which something is most powerful or successful. Opposite: Nadir, Bottom, Base, Depth
